preferred_translation: discerns it as it has come to be
alternative_translations: clearly knows it as it has come to be
definition: Formula record for pajānāti in the yathābhūta family, preserving the sharper discerning verb and the conditioned-process force of bhūta.
notes: This formula inherits the pajānāti and yathābhūta-ñāṇadassana policies. MN 1 supports the formula surface directly. Keep the discerning force explicit and do not flatten the line into generic objectivity or realization-language.

example_phrases.translation: one discerns it as it has come to be
example_phrases.notes: Core formula surface for the sharper verbal knowing family.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the yathābhūtaṃ pajānāti formula as a sharpened verbal knowing expression.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'discerns it as it has come to be' as the default rendering when this formula is translated directly.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Use the shorter alternate only when English flow demands it. Do not flatten the formula into generic objectivity or realization-language.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the formula from erasing either the discerning verbal force of pajānāti or the conditioned-process force of yathābhūta.
